title: T-013 — API: BOM create draft (POST /api/technical/items/:code/boms)
prompt:
# T-013 — API: BOM create draft (POST /api/technical/items/:code/boms)

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §7.1, §7.4, §7.6
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
BOM create with cycle detection, allocation sum validation, version bump, all transactional.

Cycle detection: DFS from current item over active BOMs in same org; component_item_ids are nodes. For self-reference reject early. Allocation guard rounds to 3 decimals. V-TEC-11 returns warning array in response (not 422).

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/items/[item_code]/boms/route.ts [create]
- lib/technical/bom/service.ts [create]
- lib/technical/bom/cycle-detection.ts [create]
- tests/api/bom-create.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- BOM approve workflow (T-014)
- Generator

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given item A's BOM where line component is item A, when POST runs, then 422 V-TEC-13.
2. Given allocations 80+15+0 (byproduct excluded), when POST runs, then 200 because parent 80 + co-product 15 = 95 — must FAIL with 422 V-TEC-12 because non-byproduct sum != 100; coverage sum = 95.
3. Given component item.status='blocked', when POST runs, then 422 V-TEC-14.
4. Given valid BOM, when POST runs, then row written and version = previous_max + 1.

## Test strategy
- RED tests for each V-TEC-12/13/14 path.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/api/bom-create.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not auto-publish (status stays draft).
- Do not write a snapshot here — snapshot is WO-time only (T-024).
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
